parsed_questions.error_comment: Task requires drawing chemical structures and curved-arrow mechanisms and is not text-oriented

parsed_questions.question_text: Consider the structure of raffinose, a trisaccharide found in sugar beets, beans, cabbage, and other plants. Classify raffinose as a reducing or nonreducing sugar, and use abbreviated structures to support your claim. The structure shows that raffinose is a non-reducing sugar, evidenced by its lack of hemiacetal carbons (highlighted in the structure). Treatment with Br2 at pH 5-6 results in no reaction, indicating the acetal carbons are not oxidizable. Bonus: Name one of the constituent monosaccharides found within raffinose. Options: D-glucose, D-galactose, or D-fructose.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: Raffinose is a non-reducing sugar which is evidenced by its lack of hemiacetal carbons; Bonus: D-glucose, D-galactose, or D-fructose

parsed_questions.error_comment: Task requires drawing and is not text-oriented

parsed_questions.question_text: A) Classify the following aldose as D or L. The aldose structures are shown with aldehyde group at the top and hydroxyl and hydrogen substituents along the carbon chain, with CH3 at the bottom. (2 pts) B) Which pair of the following aldoses are epimers and which pair are enantiomers? Aldoses A, B, and C are shown as Fischer projections with various arrangements of OH and H groups along the carbon chain. Compounds A and C are epimers. Compounds A and B are enantiomers. Bonus: Which pair of compounds are diastereomers? Choose B and C or A and C. (1 pt)
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) L; B) Compounds A and C are epimers; Compounds A and B are enantiomers; Bonus: B and C

parsed_questions.question_text: (+)-Trehalose, C12H22O11, is a non-reducing sugar found in young mushrooms and is the energy source for certain flying insects. It gives only D-glucose when hydrolyzed by aqueous acid or by α-glucosidase. It is inert to β-glucosidase. Methylation with excess methyl iodide gives an octa-O-methyl derivative that, upon hydrolysis, yields only 2,3,4,6-tetra-O-methyl-D-glucose. Explain the experimental data with annotated chemical structures. What is the structure of (+)-trehalose? Represent your answer using a Haworth representation. Bonus: Provide a common name for the polysaccharide shown on the cover of the quiz.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: The structure of (+)-trehalose is a disaccharide composed of two D-glucose units linked by an α-1,1-glycosidic bond, making it a non-reducing sugar that is inert to β-glucosidase and hydrolyzed to D-glucose by acid or α-glucosidase; it is represented by two D-glucose monosaccharides connected via the α-1,1 linkage in a Haworth structure. Bonus answer: starch

parsed_questions.question_text: An aldopentose, O, is oxidized to a diacid, P, which is optically active. Compound O is also Ruff-degraded to an aldotetrose, Q, which undergoes oxidation to an optically inactive diacid, R. Assuming that O has the D-configuration, what are the structures of O, P, Q, and R? Represent your answers in each box by using Fischer projections displayed in the standard carbohydrate orientation. Bonus: List the reagent(s) necessary to effect Ruff degradation of compound O to compound Q.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: compound O = CHO-CHOH-HOH-HOH-CH2OH (Fischer projection with OH on right on second carbon, left on third, right on fourth); compound P = COOH-CHOH-HOH-HOH-COOH (oxidized diacid form); compound Q = CHO-HOH-HOH-CH2OH (aldotetrose with OH on right on second and third carbons); compound R = COOH-HOH-HOH-COOH (oxidized diacid of Q); Bonus = 1) Br2, H2O (optionally including Ca(OH)2 or CaCO3) and 2) H2O2, Fe2SO4 or H2O2, Fe(OAc)3
